tools.name: get_cancel_policies
tools.description:
Get cancel policies for a restaurant before making a reservation.

Always call this tool before create_reservation or create_reservation_request to inform the user about cancellation terms.

IMPORTANT - For RESERVATION REQUESTS (create_reservation_request):
Before showing the cancel policy, you MUST first explain how reservation requests work:
"How does it work?
We will check the availability based on the information you provide below.
- If available, we will reserve immediately, charge your credit card, and send you a confirmation email.
- If not available, we will notify you by email. No charge will be made.
* You can only cancel a request before we confirm the availability with the restaurants (within 10 minutes ~ 24 hours of the request)."

Then introduce the cancel policy with:
"Once your reservation request is confirmed and a booking is made, the following cancellation policy will apply:"

CRITICAL - BOOKING FEE NOTICE (MUST ALWAYS DISPLAY):
When displaying cancellation policies to users, you MUST ALWAYS include this notice at the end:
"* Note: The booking fee is non-refundable regardless of cancellation timing."
This notice is MANDATORY and must be shown every time you display cancellation policies, whether for direct reservations or reservation requests. NEVER skip this notice.

Returns:
- policies: Array of cancel policy tiers (days_before, cancel_fee_percentage)
- no_show_fee_percentage: Fee for no-show
- booking_fee_note: MUST be displayed to user - booking fee is non-refundable

restaurant_id.description: Restaurant ID to get cancel policies for

course_id.description: Course external ID (optional, for course-specific policies)

tools.name: create_reservation
tools.description:
Create a direct reservation for an available opening.

REQUIRED FLOW - Follow these steps in order:

STEP 1: CANCEL POLICY - Before collecting user information:
- Call get_cancel_policies to retrieve the cancellation terms
- Explain the cancel policy to the user clearly
- MUST include: "* Note: The booking fee is non-refundable regardless of cancellation timing."
- Ensure the user understands the cancellation fees before proceeding

STEP 2: COLLECT EMAIL ONLY - First, ask ONLY for email address:
"To proceed with the reservation, please provide your email address."
- Do NOT ask for name, country, or phone yet
- Wait for the user to provide their email

STEP 3: CHECK USER & COLLECT ADDITIONAL INFO IF NEEDED:
After receiving the email, call this tool with only the email.
- If user EXISTS: The API will proceed with the existing user info. No additional info needed.
- If user DOES NOT EXIST: The API will return an error with code "USER_NOT_FOUND".
  In this case, ask the user for additional information:
  "It looks like you don't have a TableAll account yet. To create one, please provide:
  - Full name (first and last name)
  - Country
  - Phone number"
  Then call this tool again with all the information.
  When a new account is created, the API will return password_reset_url.
  You MUST display this to the user:
  "Your TableAll account has been created! Please set your password using this link: [password_reset_url]
  Note: The same link has also been sent to your email address."

STEP 4: CONFIRMATION - After collecting all information, you MUST:
1. Display a summary of all reservation details to the user:
   - Restaurant name
   - Date and time
   - Number of guests
   - Course name
   - Email address (highlight this for verification)
   - Cancel policy summary
2. Ask the user to confirm: "Please verify the information above, especially your email address. Shall I proceed with this reservation?"
3. Only call this tool AFTER the user explicitly confirms.

RESERVATION RULES:
- Use this only when an opening_id is available from check_availability
- For fixed-seat openings, party_size MUST equal the fixed_seats value
- For flexible-seat openings, party_size must be within min/max range

This tool internally:
1. Verifies or creates user account (verify_or_create)
2. Creates the reservation
3. Returns a payment URL for the user to complete

The user must open the payment_url in their browser to complete the reservation.
Note: The same payment link has also been sent to the email address provided.
Note: This payment link is valid for 30 minutes.

CRITICAL - ACCESS TOKEN HANDLING:
The create response contains "data.access_token". You MUST store this value.
It is required when calling get_reservation_status to check the reservation status.
Without the access_token, the status API will return 403 Forbidden.

RESPONSE HANDLING - NEW USER CREATED (CRITICAL):
When the response contains "user.is_new_user": true, you MUST display this message to the user:
"Since this email address was not registered, we have created a new TableAll account for you.
Please click the link below to set your password: [password_reset_url]
Note: This link is valid for 24 hours. The same link has also been sent to your email."
This message is MANDATORY when is_new_user is true. NEVER skip this message.

first_name.description: User first name / 名 (required for new users or users with incomplete profile)

last_name.description: User last name / 姓 (required for new users or users with incomplete profile)

country.description: Country code ISO 3166-1 alpha-2, e.g., "JP", "US" (required for new users or users with incomplete profile)

phone_country_code.description: Phone country code, e.g., "+81" (required for new users or users with incomplete profile)

phone.description: Phone number without country code (required for new users or users with incomplete profile)

party_size.description: Number of guests. Must match fixed_seats for fixed openings, or be within min/max range for flexible openings.

special_request.description: Special requests or notes for the restaurant

ai_client.description: AI client name. You MUST provide your AI model name (e.g., "Claude", "Gemini", "GPT", "Copilot").

tools.name: get_reservation_status
tools.description:
Check the status of a reservation by session ID.

Requires the access_token that was returned in the create_reservation response.
You MUST have stored the access_token from the create response to use this tool.
Without a valid access_token, the API will return 403 Forbidden.

IMPORTANT - PRICE DISPLAY:
The "price" field already INCLUDES the "booking_fee". Do NOT add them together.
When displaying to the user, show: price (total) and booking_fee (included in price, non-refundable).
Example: "Total: ¥30,000 (includes ¥1,000 non-refundable booking fee)"

session_id.description: Reservation session ID returned from create_reservation

access_token.description: Access token returned from create_reservation response (data.access_token). Required for authorization.

tools.name: create_reservation_request
tools.description:
Create a reservation request when no openings are available.

REQUIRED FLOW - Follow these steps in order:

STEP 1: EXPLAIN HOW RESERVATION REQUEST WORKS - First, explain to the user:
"How does it work?
We will check the availability based on the information you provide below.
- If available, we will reserve immediately, charge your credit card, and send you a confirmation email.
- If not available, we will notify you by email. No charge will be made.
* You can only cancel a request before we confirm the availability with the restaurants (within 10 minutes ~ 24 hours of the request)."

STEP 2: CANCEL POLICY - After explaining how it works:
- Call get_cancel_policies to retrieve the cancellation terms
- Explain: "Once your reservation request is confirmed and a booking is made, the following cancellation policy will apply:"
- Display the cancel policy clearly
- MUST include: "* Note: The booking fee is non-refundable regardless of cancellation timing."
- Ensure the user understands these fees apply AFTER the reservation is confirmed

STEP 3: COLLECT GUEST COUNT - You MUST confirm the number of adults and children:
- If user only says total number (e.g., "3 people"), you MUST ask: "Will there be any children in your party?"
- If children: Ask "How many adults and how many children?"
- If no children: Confirm "So that will be [X] adults, no children. Is that correct?"
- NEVER skip this confirmation step

STEP 4: COLLECT EMAIL ONLY - First, ask ONLY for email address:
"To proceed with the reservation request, please provide your email address."
- Do NOT ask for name, country, or phone yet
- Wait for the user to provide their email

STEP 5: CHECK USER & COLLECT ADDITIONAL INFO IF NEEDED:
After receiving the email, call this tool with only the email.
- If user EXISTS: The API will proceed with the existing user info. No additional info needed.
- If user DOES NOT EXIST: The API will return an error with code "USER_NOT_FOUND".
  In this case, ask the user for additional information:
  "It looks like you don't have a TableAll account yet. To create one, please provide:
  - Full name (first and last name)
  - Country
  - Phone number"
  Then call this tool again with all the information.
  When a new account is created, the API will return password_reset_url.
  You MUST display this to the user:
  "Your TableAll account has been created! Please set your password using this link: [password_reset_url]
  Note: The same link has also been sent to your email address."

STEP 6: CONFIRMATION - After collecting all information, you MUST:
1. Display a summary of all reservation request details to the user:
   - Restaurant name
   - Preferred date(s) and time range (Japan Time)
   - Number of adults
   - Number of children
   - Course name
   - Email address (highlight this for verification)
2. Ask the user to confirm: "Please verify the information above, especially your email address. Shall I proceed with this reservation request?"
3. Only call this tool AFTER the user explicitly confirms.

RESERVATION REQUEST RULES:
- Use this when check_availability returns no results
- Allows specifying multiple preferred dates
- Restaurant will review and approve/decline the request

DATE HANDLING - CRITICAL:
- When user says "any day in [month]" (e.g., "any day in January"), include ALL days of that month
- When user says "any day during [period]" (e.g., "any day from Jan 10-20"), include ALL days in that range
- When user says "anytime in [week/period]", include ALL days in that period
- Do NOT arbitrarily narrow down dates - include every day the user indicated as acceptable
- Example: "Any day in January" → include all 31 days (2025-01-01 to 2025-01-31)
- Example: "Jan 15-20 any day is fine" → include all 6 days (2025-01-15 to 2025-01-20)

This tool internally:
1. Verifies or creates user account (verify_or_create)
2. Creates the reservation request
3. Returns a payment URL for card registration

The user must open the payment_url to register their card. Payment is only charged when the restaurant approves the request.
Note: The same payment link has also been sent to the email address provided.
Note: This payment link is valid for 30 minutes.

CRITICAL - ACCESS TOKEN HANDLING:
The create response contains "data.access_token". You MUST store this value.
It is required when calling get_reservation_request_status to check the request status.
Without the access_token, the status API will return 403 Forbidden.

RESPONSE HANDLING - NEW USER CREATED (CRITICAL):
When the response contains "user.is_new_user": true, you MUST display this message to the user:
"Since this email address was not registered, we have created a new TableAll account for you.
Please click the link below to set your password: [password_reset_url]
Note: This link is valid for 24 hours. The same link has also been sent to your email."
This message is MANDATORY when is_new_user is true. NEVER skip this message.

tools.name: purchaseElicitation
tools.description: Interactive, stateful endpoint to guide small-footprint AI agents through a purchase flow for gift cards or mobile top-ups. Call repeatedly with the same session_token until 'status' is 'complete' or 'error'. For the first call, provide an empty 'body': {} (session_token will be generated and returned). Provide 'answer' in the body to respond to the current question. Use 'action: back' in the body to go to the previous step. Handles product type selection, country, brand, amount, beneficiary, coin, network, user email, and final confirmation/validation/order creation.

session_token.description: Unique token for the session (returned in first response; required for subsequent calls).

answer.description: User's response to the current question (e.g., 'giftcard', 'IT', 'Amazon.it', '50', 'user@example.com', 'USDC', 'SOLANA', 'yes').

action.description: Optional action like 'back' to revert to the previous step.

price.description: Display label for fixed denomination (e.g., '5 EUR', '3 months subscription', '100 diamonds + 10 free').

oneOf.description: User pays directly from an on-chain wallet

MultiProductsOrderResponse.description: Full payload returned after creating an order or polling its status.

order_id.description: UUID v4 that uniquely identifies the order

created_at.description: Unix epoch (seconds) when the order was created

qr_url.description: uri to retrieve a QR code that can be used to start the payment

checkout_url.description: when present, redirect the use here to start the payment

payment_transactions.description: Blockchain TX hashes or processor IDs

oneOf.description: URL the user must open to reveal / activate the code.

pin_serial.description: Serial number (shown on some physical/virtual cards).

security_code.description: Extra security / CVV code if required by the brand.

pin_usage_instructions.description: HTML instructions on how to redeem the code.

beneficiary_account.description: A string formatted as an email (if delivery_type requires email) or a phone number (if delivery_type requires phone).

oneOf.description: Email format when delivery_type indicates email-based delivery.
Phone number format (E.164, up to 15 digits, optional leading +) when delivery_type indicates phone-based delivery.

generate_audio.description: Generate audio/voice for the video. Default: true

tools.description: Check the status of a video generation order. Use this to poll for completion after creating a video. Returns status (processing/completed/failed) and download URL when ready.

tools.name: get_match_predictions
tools.description: Get AI-powered predictions for Premier League and Champions League matches including half-time score, full-time score, next goal scorer, and corner predictions. Real-time predictions available in FootballBin iOS app.

league.description: League to get predictions for. Accepts: "premier_league", "epl", "pl", "champions_league", "ucl", "cl"

matchweek.description: Matchweek number (optional, defaults to current matchweek)

home_team.description: Filter by home team name (optional, e.g., "chelsea", "arsenal", "man_utd")

away_team.description: Filter by away team name (optional, e.g., "liverpool", "wolves")

tools.name: docs_search
tools.description: Search across Avalanche documentation, academy courses, integrations, and blog posts

query.description: The search query

limit.description: Maximum number of results (default: 10)

tools.name: docs_fetch
tools.description: Fetch a specific documentation page as markdown

url.description: The page URL path (e.g., /docs/primary-network/overview)

tools.name: docs_list_sections
tools.description: List available documentation sections and their page counts

tools.description: Get the native AVAX balance of an address on C-Chain

chainId.description: Chain ID — "43114" for C-Chain mainnet, "43113" for Fuji testnet

tools.name: blockchain_get_contract_info
tools.description: Check if an address is a contract and get its ERC20 name/symbol if applicable

chainId.description: Chain ID — "43114" for C-Chain mainnet, "43113" for Fuji testnet

tools.name: blockchain_lookup_transaction
tools.description: Look up a transaction by hash on Avalanche (C-Chain, P-Chain, or X-Chain). Supports 0x format (C-Chain) and CB58 format (P/X-Chain).

txHash.description: Transaction hash (0x... for C-Chain, CB58 for P/X-Chain)

tools.name: blockchain_lookup_address
tools.description: Look up an address — balance, contract info. Use when users paste an 0x address.

address.description: The address to look up (0x format)

chainId.description: Chain ID — "43114" for C-Chain mainnet, "43113" for Fuji testnet

tools.name: blockchain_lookup_subnet
tools.description: Look up a Subnet / L1 by its ID — validators, chains, and configuration.

tools.name: blockchain_lookup_chain
tools.description: Look up a blockchain by its ID — name, VM type, and subnet.

tools.name: blockchain_lookup_validator
tools.description: Look up a validator by node ID — stake, uptime, delegation info.

tools.name: github_search_code
tools.description: Search for code across Avalanche GitHub repositories (avalanchego, icm-services, builders-hub).

query.description: The search query string to find relevant code.

repo.description: The repository to search in. Defaults to "all".

language.description: Filter results by programming language. Defaults to "any".

tools.name: github_get_file
tools.description: Retrieve the contents of a specific file from an Avalanche GitHub repository.

repo.description: The repository to fetch the file from (owner is always ava-labs).

path.description: The path to the file within the repository.

ref.description: The git ref (branch, tag, or commit SHA) to fetch from. Defaults to "HEAD".

tools.description: Get the AVAX balance of one or more P-Chain addresses

tools.name: info_get_node_version
tools.description: Get the version of the node, including the database version, git commit, and API compatibility info.

network.description: The Avalanche network to query (default: mainnet)

tools.name: info_get_network_id
tools.description: Get the numeric ID of the Avalanche network this node is participating in.

network.description: The Avalanche network to query (default: mainnet)

tools.name: info_get_network_name
tools.description: Get the human-readable name of the Avalanche network this node is participating in.

network.description: The Avalanche network to query (default: mainnet)

tools.name: info_get_blockchain_id
tools.description: Get the CB58-encoded blockchain ID for a given blockchain alias (e.g., "X", "P", "C").

alias.description: The blockchain alias (e.g., "X", "P", "C", or a full blockchain name)

network.description: The Avalanche network to query (default: mainnet)

tools.name: info_is_bootstrapped
tools.description: Check whether a given chain has finished bootstrapping on the node.

chain.description: The chain ID or alias to check (e.g., "X", "P", "C")

network.description: The Avalanche network to query (default: mainnet)

tools.name: info_get_tx_fee
tools.description: Get the current transaction fees for the network, returned in both nAVAX and AVAX.

network.description: The Avalanche network to query (default: mainnet)

tools.name: info_peers
tools.description: Get a list of peers this node is connected to. Optionally filter by specific NodeIDs.

nodeIDs.description: Optional list of NodeIDs to filter peers by (e.g., ["NodeID-AbC..."]). If omitted, all peers are returned.

network.description: The Avalanche network to query (default: mainnet)

tools.name: info_acps
tools.description: Get information about Avalanche Community Proposals (ACPs), including their status and vote counts.

network.description: The Avalanche network to query (default: mainnet)

tools.name: get-token-metadata
tools.description: Returns the metadata for a given token address stored in the database. Social fields are omitted.

tokenAddress.description: The token's address (e.g., a Solana address)

tools.name: get-token-marketdata
tools.description: Returns real-time metrics including price, market capitalization, FDV, liquidity, total and circulating supply, and current holder count. This endpoint does not use time windows and does not include trade statistics.

tokenAddress.description: The token's mint address (e.g., a Solana SPL token address)

tools.name: get-token-holders
tools.description: Returns ranked holder wallets, amounts, and percentage ownership for a given token address.

limit.description: Optional limit on number of holders returned (default: 25, max: 100)

tokenAddress.description: The token's mint address (e.g., Solana token address)

tools.name: get-trending-tokens
tools.description: Returns a list of trending tokens ordered by transaction count for a given interval.

interval.description: Time interval window for counting transactions (default: 5m)

tools.name: search-tokens
tools.description: Performs a flexible search for tokens by name, symbol, or address, with optional filters, sorting, and symbol-only mode.

searchQuery.description: Query text to search in name, symbol, or address

limit.description: Number of results to return (1–25)

tools.name: get-latest-graduated-tokens
tools.description: Fetches the most recently graduated tokens, ordered by graduation time (descending).

limit.description: Number of results to return (1–100). Defaults to 25.

tools.name: get-token-ath
tools.description: Returns the all-time-high (ATH) market cap and the timestamp when it occurred, based on all historical trades for the token.

tokenAddress.description: The token's mint address (Solana SPL token).

tools.name: get-tokens-by-deployer
tools.description: Returns a list of tokens where the specified wallet address is the developer (devWallet).

limit.description: Maximum number of tokens to return (default: 10, min: 1, max: 100)

walletAddress.description: The wallet address of the deployer (devWallet)

tools.name: get-token-history
tools.description: Fetches historical price, market cap, or liquidity snapshots for a token across multiple time windows.

tools.name: get-token-performance
tools.description: Returns percentage performance change for selected metrics over multiple time windows compared to the current value.

token.description: Token address to fetch performance data for

metrics.description: List of metrics to evaluate. Supported values: `price`, `mcap`, `liquidity`.

tools.name: get-token-price
tools.description: Returns the latest price, market cap, and liquidity for a specific token address.

tools.name: get-wallet-holdings
tools.description: Retrieve the token holdings of a given wallet address, including balances and percentage ownership per token.

limit.description: Maximum number of tokens to return (default 10, max 100)

walletAddress.description: The wallet address to fetch holdings for

tools.name: get-wallet-token-pnl
tools.description: Calculate realized and unrealized profit and loss (PnL) for a wallet on a specific token.

walletAddress.description: The wallet address to calculate PnL for

tools.name: get-token-history-range
tools.description: Returns the lowest and highest values of selected metrics (price, market cap, liquidity) within a specified time range for a token.

metrics.description: Which metrics to return (price, mcap, liquidity)

mode.description: Behavior when requested range falls outside token lifespan

tools.name: get-mcp-config
tools.description: Exposes MCP discovery schema for Smithery and other MCP clients.

tools.name: get-token-audit
tools.description: Returns audit data for a given token address, including developer, top holder, insider, bundler, sniper, and LP burn distributions.

tokenAddress.description: The token's address (e.g., a Solana mint address)

tools.name: get-api-health
tools.description: Returns 200 OK if the API service is healthy and responding.

tools.name: get-pair-audit
tools.description: Returns audit data for a single liquidity pair and its underlying token. Includes token-level distribution (developer, top holders, insiders, bundlers) and pair-level ownership breakdown (snipers, LP burn).

pairAddress.description: The pair address (e.g., liquidity pool address)

tools.name: get-token-audit-multiple
tools.description:
Returns audit data for multiple tokens in a single batch call. Accepts up to 10 token addresses via a comma-separated `addresses` query parameter. Each entry includes developer, top holder, insider, bundler, sniper, and LP burn distributions.

**Cost:** 10 credits per request.

addresses.description: Comma-separated list of up to 10 token mint addresses

tools.name: get-pair-audit-multiple
tools.description:
Returns audit data for multiple liquidity pairs in a single batch call. Accepts up to 10 pair addresses via the `addresses` query parameter. Each entry includes pair-level and token-level audit details including developer, top holders, insiders, bundlers, snipers, and LP burn distribution.

**Cost:** 10 credits per request.

addresses.description: Comma-separated list of up to 10 pair addresses

tools.name: get_user_profile
tools.description: Fetches the **current user's profile** whenever user asks something about themselves — even vague or general phrases like 'tell me about me', 'who am I', or 'what do you know about me'. Call this tool as long as the user refers to themselves.

tools.name: findleadforprovider
tools.description: Use this tool ANY TIME the user asks about find services, leads or find work for me or user finding client.  Positive trigger examples: • 'I want work related to AWS billing' • 'suggest services for me' • 'find clients for me' • 'find leads'

keywords.description: List of keywords extracted from the user's query, e.g. ['web development project', 'AWS', 'frontend', 'backend']

limit.description: Maximum number of profiles to return. keep Defaults to 5 if not provided.

tools.name: suggestservices
tools.description: Use this tool ANY TIME the user asks about services, service providers, offerings, consultants, freelancers, agencies, experts, or skills available for hire. Call if the user says 'I need someone who can...', 'Find me a service provider for...', 'Suggest freelancers/agencies', 'Who can do AWS bill cutting', etc.  Positive trigger examples: • 'I need a Webdeveloper.' • 'I want someone to help me with Database management' • 'Find me AWS billing consultant' • 'Suggest digital marketing agency for startups'  Negative examples (DO NOT CALL): • 'Explain how your service embeddings work' (no provider intent)

keywords.description: List of keywords extracted from the user's query, e.g. ['AWS', 'billing', 'consultant', 'Delhi']

filters.description: Optional filters to narrow down service providers.

searchText.description: Describe user requirement in 50 to 100 word

serviceTitle.description: On the basis of requirenment write service title

location.description: If user specify location then write location e.g. city , country, state, region or by default keep it null.

ets.description: On the basis of requirenment write the estimate time required in days and hours e.g. 2 days 3 hours to solve the requirement

aproximateBudget.description: On the basis of requirenment  the take the approximate budget from the user in users currency.

limit.description: Number of results to return (default 5)

tools.name: save_linkedin_profile_url
tools.description: Use this tool to save the linkedin profile url in profiles db. Whenever user provide linkedin profile url then save it without asking,

tools.name: addservices
tools.description: Use this tool when the user wants to add, create, or register their own services as a provider. Call if the user says things like: • 'I want to register my service' • 'Add my consulting services to the platform' • 'Publish my offerings' or 'Save my service details'  Do NOT call for queries about finding or suggesting service providers (use `suggestservices` for that).

services.description: List of services the user wants to add.

tools.name: checkServiceAcceptance
tools.description: Use this tool when you need to verify whether a specific service (by ID) has been accepted by the logged-in user. Call this if the user says things like: • 'Did I accept that service?' • 'Show me who requested this service.' • 'Check the service acceptance status.'  Do NOT call this for listing or suggesting services (use `suggestservices` for that).

serviceId.description: Unique ID of the service whose acceptance needs to be verified.

users.description: Array of X usernames (handles). You may include a leading '@'.
users.title: Users

mode.description: normal = fast latest-post snapshot at scale; deep = deeper per-user collection (slower).
mode.title: Mode

idempotency_key.description: Optional idempotency key for safe retries (recommended for agents).
idempotency_key.title: Idempotency Key
